title: Sankalp / Ritual Tracker
description: Schema for a user's active sankalp (ritual vow). Stored on-device in the app. Content templates live in sankalp_templates/ and are loaded when a user starts a new sankalp.

id.description: UUID generated by the app when the user starts the sankalp

name.description: Display name — either from the template or user-customized

user_sankalpa_text.description: The actual sankalpa mantra text — filled with user's gotra, name, and location at the time of sankalp

started_date.description: Date the user started the sankalp (YYYY-MM-DD)

end_date.description: Date the sankalp concludes. May be the same as started_date for single-day rituals.

duration_days.description: Number of days the sankalp spans

recurrence.description: For recurring sankalps (e.g. every Ekadashi, every Monday of Shravan)

pattern.description: once = single occurrence; monthly_tithi = repeats every month on the same tithi

repeat_count.description: How many times to repeat (e.g. 21 Mondays, 12 Ekadashis)

completed_count.description: How many repetitions have been completed so far

calendar_marks.description: Dates to mark on the app calendar for this sankalp

label.description: Short label shown on the calendar dot

status.description: Whether this specific day was completed or missed

alarms.description: Time-specific alarms set for this sankalp — e.g. brahma muhurta alarm, pradosha alarm, moonrise alarm

label.description: What the alarm is for — shown in the notification

time_type.description: How the alarm time is calculated. 'fixed_time' uses time_of_day; others are computed from panchang data.

time_of_day.description: HH:MM — used only when time_type is 'fixed_time'

offset_minutes.description: Minutes before (negative) or after (positive) the reference time. E.g. -90 for 90 min before sunrise = Brahma Muhurta

mantra_id.description: ID of the shloka/mantra to be shown when this alarm fires

active_dates.description: If set, alarm only fires on these specific dates. If empty, fires every day of the sankalp.

notification_message.description: The notification text the user sees

guide.description: The step-by-step ritual guide — loaded from the sankalp_template

preparation.description: What to do BEFORE the ritual begins (gather materials, fast preparation, etc.)

substitute.description: Acceptable substitute if the original is unavailable

action.description: What to do in this step

mantra_id.description: Shloka or mantra to chant during this step

why_this_mantra.description: Why this specific mantra is used in this ritual — the meaning in context

what_to_visualize.description: What to hold in the mind's eye while chanting

forgiveness_mantra_id.description: Reference to the Kshama Prarthana or ritual-specific forgiveness mantra

after_completion.description: Instructions for what happens after the sankalp concludes

materials_disposal.description: How to dispose of each ritual material respectfully (immerse in water, give to tree, distribute as prasad, etc.)

next_steps.description: What to do after the sankalp is complete — next ritual, follow-up vrat, etc.

udyapan.description: Udyapan = the formal conclusion ritual. Some long vrats require a specific udyapan puja to properly close the vow.

user_notes.description: User's personal notes about their sankalp experience

missed_days.description: Dates when the user missed the ritual. Used to suggest the appropriate Kshama Prarthana.
